agency: New York City Department of Finance (DOF)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov/robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/finance (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform + Dynatrace) and the legacy a836-*.nyc.gov application layer: ACRIS (a836-acris.nyc.gov, ASP.NET/IIS, iso-8859-1 meta-refresh framesets, Last-Modified 2013), CityPay (a836-citypay.nyc.gov, a PayPal/Braintree-backed payment form per its CSP), and the Property Tax System access portal (a836-pts-access.nyc.gov, session-cookie/Akamai gated). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Department of Finance (DOF)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 145 assets with column schemas.

meta.platform: NYC.gov 'Livesite' informational chassis (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace) plus a fleet of aging, siloed a836-*.nyc.gov apps — ACRIS (ASP.NET/IIS, 2013-era), CityPay (PayPal/Braintree form), and the Property Tax System.
meta.netnew: ParkingTicketPayment — pay a parking/camera violation via API instead of the CityPay-only web form.
meta.tagline: DOF publishes more open data than any NYC agency assessed, yet every citizen transaction lives behind a fleet of aging, API-less a836 legacy apps.
meta.headline: The fifth pattern: the reference data is wide open (145 datasets) but the transaction layer is trapped in legacy application silos with no API — modernize the a836 app fleet behind one owned contract.

scorecard_notes.platform: NYC.gov Livesite chassis + a fleet of legacy a836 apps (ACRIS ASP.NET/IIS 2013, CityPay, PTS) — dated but in-house.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content or site API; ACRIS/CityPay/PTS are server-rendered apps, not APIs — no OpenAPI, no JSON.
scorecard_notes.openData: Widest footprint assessed — 145 DOF datasets across valuation, ACRIS, exemptions, tax charges, and parking.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Every core entity (valuation, exemptions, tax-charge balance, ACRIS documents, parking violations) has a rich, machine-readable Open Data twin.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No payment/transaction API — paying a ticket or tax bill exists only inside the CityPay/PTS web apps.
scorecard_notes.agent: No MCP, no agent-native surface; the legacy apps are browser-only.
scorecard_notes.ownership: DOF owns its systems and data in-house (ACRIS, CityPay, PTS built for the city; data on Socrata) — not rented to a SaaS vendor.

headline_findings: DOF has the widest open-data footprint of any NYC agency assessed so far — 145 NYC Open Data datasets covering property valuation & assessment, exemptions/abatements, tax-charge balances, the full ACRIS document register, and every parking/camera violation.

But its citizen transactions live across a fleet of aging, siloed a836-*.nyc.gov apps — ACRIS (a 2013-era ASP.NET/IIS document-search app), CityPay (a PayPal/Braintree web form), and the Property Tax System — none of which exposes an API, OpenAPI, or JSON contract.

This is the fifth distinct pattern: not data-as-HTML (Parks), not rented search (DOE), not fragmented-but-real APIs (Council), not one locked vendor CRM (NYCHA) — but a spread of in-house legacy application silos sitting on top of an unusually open data layer.

The reference model is coherent and joinable on the Borough-Block-Lot (BBL): valuation, exemptions, tax charges, and ACRIS legals all key on it. Publishing that as one owned resource model spares consumers 145 Socrata IDs and three legacy screens.

Net-new write surface: pay a parking/camera violation (ParkingTicketPayment) — the balance is already published (Open Parking and Camera Violations, nc67-uf89), but the act of paying has no API; it lives only in CityPay. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 145 DOF datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open DOF API — valuation, exemptions, tax charges, ACRIS register, parking violations. Reference/snapshot data only.

ACRIS — deeds, mortgages, parties, legals. iso-8859-1 meta-refresh framesets to /CP/, X-Powered-By: ASP.NET, Last-Modified 2013. Server-rendered; no JSON/OpenAPI. Open Data twin exists (bnx9-e6tj et al.) but the live app is API-less.
Pay parking tickets, property taxes, and other charges. CSP allowlists js.braintreegateway.com and *.paypal.com — a PayPal/Braintree-backed form. No public payment API. The net-new write surface.
Property Tax System account access; Akamai-fronted, session-cookie gated (mspwvw-* cookie). Server-rendered; no API.
Content only — About, property, benefits, how-to. Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM, livesite-version: 22. No content API.

fruit.name: Property Valuation & Assessment
opendata_match: Property Valuation and Assessment Data yjxr-fw8i (45c)
Property Valuation and Assessment Data Tax Classes 1,2,3,4 8y4t-faws (139c)
fruit.notes: The TaxLot valuation record — assessed/market values, lot dimensions, tax class. Keyed on BBLE (Borough-Block-Lot-Easement); full geography spine.

fruit.name: Property Exemptions & Abatements
fruit.notes: STAR/SCHE/J-51/421-a/ICIP benefit lines per parcel; keyed on PARID (BBL) + year + exemption code.

fruit.name: Property Tax Charges / Balance
fruit.machine_readable: Open Data (SODA) — snapshot; live account in PTS/CityPay
opendata_match: Property Tax Rates by Tax Class 7zb8-7bpk (5c)
fruit.notes: Outstanding liability/interest/balance-due per parcel account; keyed on PARID (BBL) + tax year. The live account and payment surface lives in the PTS and CityPay apps.

fruit.name: ACRIS Recorded Documents
fruit.notes: Deeds, mortgages, satisfactions, UCCs — master + legals (affected lots) + parties (grantor/grantee). Keyed on Document ID / CRFN. System of record is the legacy a836-acris.nyc.gov app.

fruit.name: Parking & Camera Violations
opendata_match: Open Parking and Camera Violations nc67-uf89 (19c)
Parking Violations Issued - Fiscal Year 2026 pvqr-7yc4 (44c)
fruit.notes: Issuance record (pvqr-7yc4) plus the live status-and-balance record (nc67-uf89) with Amount Due. Keyed on Summons Number. The balance is open; paying is not.

fruit.name: Pay a parking / camera ticket
opendata_match: Open Parking and Camera Violations nc67-uf89 (balance twin, read-only)
fruit.notes: The net-new WRITE surface. Paying a ticket lives only in the CityPay web app (PayPal/Braintree) or by mail; no payment API. The balance is already published, but the act of paying has no machine-readable contract.

fruit.name: Pay a property tax bill
fruit.notes: Property tax payment lives only in CityPay / the PTS portal; no API. An alternative net-new write surface (PropertyTaxPayment).

fruit.name: Record / search a real-property document
fruit.notes: ACRIS document search and e-recording run inside a 2013-era ASP.NET/IIS app; the Open Data mirror is read-only and lagged. No API for the live register.

fruit.name: Digital Tax Map
opendata_match: Department of Finance Digital Tax Map smk3-tmxj
fruit.notes: DTM — authoritative parcel geometry keyed on BBL. Underpins every valuation and ACRIS legal record.
